admission_data.chief_complaint: Chest tightness and chest pain for 3 days
admission_data.present_illness_history: Patient presented 3 days ago with substernal dull pain without obvious precipitating factors, crushing in nature, radiating to left shoulder and left upper limb, lasting about 10-15 minutes, relieved by rest. Accompanied by palpitations and dyspnea, no syncope. Presented to our emergency department, ECG showed V1-V4 leads ST elevation, elevated cardiac enzymes, admitted with diagnosis of acute anterior wall myocardial infarction.
admission_data.past_medical_history: Hypertension for 10 years, highest blood pressure 160/95mmHg, taking amlodipine 5mg qd, blood pressure fairly well controlled. Type 2 diabetes mellitus for 5 years, taking metformin 0.5g bid, blood glucose control fair. Denies history of hepatitis, tuberculosis, or other infectious diseases.
admission_data.physical_examination: T 36.5C, P 88 bpm, R 20 breaths/min, BP 135/85mmHg. Alert, fair general condition. Bilateral lung sounds clear, no rales. Heart rate 88 bpm, regular rhythm, normal heart sounds, no pathological murmurs at any valve area. Abdomen soft, non-tender. No lower extremity edema.
admission_diagnosis: Coronary heart disease - Acute anterior wall ST-elevation myocardial infarction

hospital_course.treatment_summary: Patient was immediately placed on the chest pain center green pathway upon admission, and pre-procedural workup was completed. Emergency coronary angiography was performed 2 hours after admission, showing 95% stenosis of the proximal left anterior descending artery, and one drug-eluting stent was implanted. Post-procedure, patient received dual antiplatelet therapy (aspirin 100mg qd + clopidogrel 75mg qd), statin for lipid management, ACEI for ventricular remodeling, and beta-blocker for heart rate control. Hospital course was uneventful, no chest pain episodes, no complications.
procedures_performed: 2025-01-28 Coronary artery stent implantation (left anterior descending artery)
hospital_course.significant_findings: Coronary angiography: Left main artery no significant stenosis; proximal LAD 95% stenosis, TIMI flow grade 2; mid left circumflex artery 30% stenosis; right coronary artery no significant stenosis. Post-procedure TIMI flow grade 3. Cardiac enzymes: CK-MB peak 186U/L, cTnI peak 12.5ng/mL. Echocardiography: Left ventricular anterior wall hypokinesis, EF 52%.

discharge_diagnosis: Coronary heart disease - Acute anterior wall ST-elevation myocardial infarction
discharge_status.discharge_condition: Improved. Patient has no chest pain, no chest tightness, no shortness of breath. Good spirits and appetite, sleeping well, normal bowel and bladder function. Physical examination: vital signs stable, cardiopulmonary auscultation unremarkable, no lower extremity edema.

follow_up.instructions: Take medications regularly, do not discontinue without medical advice. Low-salt, low-fat, diabetic diet. Quit smoking, limit alcohol, control weight. Monitor blood pressure and blood glucose, maintain BP <130/80mmHg, fasting glucose <7mmol/L. Moderate exercise, avoid strenuous activity and heavy physical labor. Maintain emotional stability, avoid agitation.
follow_up_appointments: Cardiology outpatient: 1 week after discharge (2025-02-12), Tuesday morning, bring discharge summary
Cardiology outpatient: 1 month after discharge, recheck complete blood count, liver and kidney function, lipid panel, ECG
Cardiology outpatient: 3-6 months after discharge, recheck echocardiography
warning_signs: Chest pain or chest tightness, especially if persistent and unrelieved for more than 15 minutes
Severe dyspnea, inability to lie flat
Gum bleeding, skin bruising (possible adverse reaction to antiplatelet medication)
Muscle soreness, fatigue (possible adverse reaction to statin medication)
follow_up.activity_restrictions: Avoid strenuous exercise and heavy physical labor for 1 month after discharge. Light activities such as walking are permitted. Resume normal activities gradually after physician evaluation at 3 months post-procedure.
follow_up.diet_instructions: Low-salt (less than 6g per day), low-fat, low-sugar diet. Eat more vegetables, fruits, whole grains, and fish. Reduce organ meats, fatty meats, and fried foods. Quit smoking and limit alcohol.
